top_signals.title: Physical Crude Cargoes Drop as Middle Eastern Supply Rises
top_signals.summary: Physical crude oil cargoes are selling at steep discounts around the world, changing trade flows as markets come under pressure from fast-rising Middle Eastern supply with Iran set to boost sales following a temporary reprieve from U.S. sanctions.The discounts…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: SK Hynix ADR Plans Leave Arb Traders Waiting on One Key Answer
top_signals.summary: SK Hynix Inc.’s planned $29 billion US listing has arbitrage investors dissecting securities filings and peppering brokers with questions about a single unresolved issue: whether the Korean chipmaker’s American depository receipts can be freely exchanged for Seoul-listed shares, a feature that could determine whether price gaps persist between the two markets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: SNB Must Stay Nimble for Hike or Cut to Negative Rate, IMF Says
top_signals.summary: The Swiss National Bank must stay ready to either raise or cut interest rates if economic circumstances require it, the International Monetary Fund said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Iran-linked entities moved $3.8B through CoinEx, TRM says
top_signals.summary: TRM Labs traced $3.8 billion moved by 60 sanctioned Iranian entities through CoinEx and said that the exchange’s 8% share of illicit transactions was substantially higher than its counterparts.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
top_signals.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Global tech stocks rebound after Micron’s profit surge
top_signals.summary: US chipmaker’s bumper quarterly profits calm investor fears after market sell-off this week

top_signals.title: Beating the market is even harder than you think
Endurance Gold hits high-grade step-outs at Reliance project
top_signals.summary: The company plans to begin a preliminary economic assessment this year.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin miners face deepening margin squeeze as revenue falls below production costs
top_signals.summary: An estimated 20% of miners are now unprofitable at current prices, and the stress shows up at the network level, too.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
top_signals.summary: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

alerts.title: Germany Scraps Frigate Program, Rheinmetall Shares Plummet
alerts.summary: Germany scrapped a landmark frigate programme on Wednesday following delays and expected cost overruns, sending shares in the country's top defence firm Rheinmetall — which was tipped to win the contract — sharply lower.Plans to build six F126 frigates had been mired in trouble for a while…
